channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Forza Horizon 6 Minamino Treasure Hunt Location: Welcome to Japan Autumn Festival Playlist
item.content:encoded:
A fresh week has begun in Forza Horizon 6, bringing a new batch of tasks to complete in the Autumn period of the Welcome to Japan Festival Playlist, including a new Treasure Hunt in the Minamino region.

The first seasonal playlist of Forza Horizon 6 kicked off on May 28 with Summer, marking the start of a four-week period of new content drops and seasonal changes in the Welcome to Japan Playlist, and now it’s time for Autumn to bring widespread changes across the map.

If you want to hunt down the new treasure chest in Forza Horizon 6, Insider Gaming has the details you need.

Where to Find the Minamino Treasure Hunt Chest in FH6 Welcome to Japan Autumn Playlist

The Treasure Hunt Chest in the Forza Horizon 6 Welcome to Japan Summer Festival Playlist in the Minamino region is located on the golf course, highlighted on the map above.

Unlike when locating a Treasure Car or Barn Find, you don’t have to manually collect the Chest, nor do you need to watch a cut scene, and, instead, you can just smash through the Chest to collect your reward, just like you do for the Mascots and Horizon XP boards on the map.

  • More Insider Gaming: All Welcome to Japan Autumn Festival Playlist Events, Challenges & Rewards

If you have already visited the location, you can fast travel straight there. If not, head to one of the nearby roads and work your way across the course until you spot the Chest, which is located at the top of a hill.

If you’re struggling to find it, keep an eye out for the location of nearby players in the area, as players tend to congregate around the location of the Treasure Hunt, making it even easier to find.

What Do You Get From The Treasure Hunt Chest?

Hunting down the Treasure Chest in the Minamino region during the Autumn season of the Forza Horizon 6 Welcome to Japan playlist is certainly worthwhile, with the main reward being a hefty chunk of 100,000 Credits.

Credits are the lifeblood of Forza Horizon 6, used for everything from buying new cars and new houses, to upgrading your favorite vehicle and customization.

  • More Insider Gaming: What is The Fastest Car in Forza Horizon 6?

While it doesn’t take too long to earn millions of Credits in Forza Horizon 6, it’s always worth making sure you have as much as possible, particularly with the regular content drops and new offerings.

The last thing you want is to have something new added to the game that you’re desperate for, only to discover you’re short on the Credits needed.

Are you enjoying the Welcome to Japan Festival Playlist in Forza Horizon 6? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

Don’t forget to tune into the Insider Gaming Showcase on May 28, featuring 55 total games, including 12 World Premieres.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/forza-horizon-6-minamino-treasure-hunt-welcome-to-japan-autumn/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: All Forza Horizon 6 Welcome to Japan Autumn Festival Playlist Events, Challenges & Rewards
item.content:encoded:
The influx of fresh content into Forza Horizon 6 continues with the second week of the Welcome to Japan Festival Playlist, as the season transitions to Autumn.

Festival Playlists in Forza Horizon 6 are spread across four weeks, each featuring a different season with map changes and challenges, before moving on to the next playlist.

For all the Challenges to complete this week and all the rewards available, Insider Gaming has the details you need.

All Welcome to Japan Autumn Festival Playlist Daily Challenges & Rewards

Daily Challenges in Forza Horizon 6 are released daily, but they remain available until the end of the current season.

As a result, you have a longer period of time to complete the initial challenges, and a shorter window for the latter ones. However, you can stack them, as you don’t have to complete them in the initial 24 hours after they release.

The seven Daily Challenges for the Autumn season in the Welcome to Japan Festival Playlist and the rewards they provide are outlined in the table below.

All Welcome to Japan Autumn Festival Playlist Weekly Challenges & Rewards

As ever, Weekly Challenges in Forza Horizon 6 are themed around a specific car, and the vehicle of focus this week is the Nissan Silvia K’s.

You’ll need to own the car to tick through the challenges, which must be completed in the set order, and the final reward is Five Festival Points and 25,000 Credits.

Each step of the Weekly Challenge in the Forza Horizon 6 Welcome to Japan Autumn Festival Playlist is detailed below.

  1. Own & Drive the 1989 Nissan Silvia K’s.
  2. Earn 3 Stars at Drift Zones in the 1989 Nissan Silvia K’s.
  3. Win a Road Race in the 1989 Nissan Silvia K’s.
  4. Complete 2 runs at the Festival Kilometre Drag Strip in the Nissan Silvia K’s.

All Welcome to Japan Autumn Festival Playlist Events & Rewards

The table below highlights all other Seasonal events and challenges in the Welcome to Japan Autumn Festival Playlist, and all the available rewards.

What do you make of these rewards? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

Don’t forget to tune into the Insider Gaming Showcase on May 28, featuring 55 total games, including 12 World Premieres

MOBILE APP

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

item.title: BeamNG.drive Releases for PS5 in 2026
item.content:encoded:
A new announcement confirms that BeamNG.drive will be released for PS5 and PS5 Pro in 2026.

BeamNG.drive is a soft-body physics driving simulator that players may have seen at some point when scrolling through social media. It provides highly satisfying crash physics and a variety of vehicles across different terrains. Many have been wondering when will BeamNG.drive release for console and the answer is this year.

BeamNG.drive Announced for PS5 and PS5 Pro, Releases This Year

A new post on the PlayStation blog reveals that BeamNG.drive will release for PS5 and PS5 Pro in 2026, however, no date or window was provided. The announcement also comes with a new trailer that interested readers can check out below:

Accurate Crash Physics

BeamNG.drive is a game that is wildly popular for it’s crash physics and that’s only possible by simulating every component of the vehicle individually as a “network of nodes and beams that can flex under load, deform, or even break outright when pushed too far.” Due to this type of sumulation, no two crashes ever feel the same, and players get “true-to-life handling dynamics with incredibly accurate damage modeling”.

The developers reveal that physics engine updates at a rate of 2kHz, which means the vehicle is fully re-evaluated two thousand times every second, making the crash physics possible. Players will see the impact propagating through the structure of the vehicle through the smallest of crashes, even hitting the curb at a wrong angle.

Thusands of Vehicles With A Lot of Customization Freedom

As BeamNG.drive is a driving sim, players do receive a varrty of vehicle configurations across different categories, including supercars, sports cars, muscle cars, SUVs, minivans, mid-90s sedans, buses, taxis, limousines, desert trucks, rock crawlers and much more. Players can also use heavy machinery to drop things like a “40-ton marble block onto an unsuspecting van.”

With all the vehicles in BeamNG.drive on PS5, players will receive tons of customisation options too, and it goes really deep. From changing the paint job, to swapping the engine, suspension type, change drivetrain layout, fit different wheels and tires, add aftermarket performance parts and so much more, the options are almost limitless. What’s more, every change will make the car feel different on the road.

Other customization options include adjustable sway bars, ride height, tire pressure, locking differentials, high/low range transfer cases, triple-bypass shocks on the off-roaders, adaptive spoilers, and more.

Lots of Different Maps

When you have access to so many vehicles and that much customisation, you also need roads and other terrains to test the vehicle and BeamNG.drive on PS5 has you covered. Players will be offered over a dozen open world maps which range from coastal, mountain, desert, industrial, and suburban environments. There’s also a mission system that allows players to try out a selection of races, time trials, police chases, and other challenges. The free roam mode will be the best for really testing out the limits of the game.

In other news, Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced will receive a PS5 Pro version, and the devs want to support features like the upgraded PSSR. Additionally, a new listing on the ESRB website suggests that Gang Beasts is receiving a port for the PS5. What are your thoughts on BeamNG.drive releasing for PS5 and PS5 Pro in 2026? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Krafton reportedly agreeing to pay Subnautica 2 devs upto $250 million. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: When Does The Witcher 3 Songs Of The Past Expansion DLC Release?
item.content:encoded:
It’s been over a decade since The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt was released, but that doesn’t mean CD Projekt Red are done with it just yet, as a new expansion has officially been announced.

Titled Songs of the Past, players will join Geralt once more before a new journey begins in The Witcher 4 with Ciri. Of course, with how good the past two expansions were, it’s got fans incredibly excited to dive back into The Witcher 3, but when should you clear your calendars for?

Here’s when the Songs of the Past expansion is due to release for The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt.

When Does The Witcher 3 Songs Of The Past Release?

All that’s been shared by CD Projekt Red is that Songs of the Past will release sometime in 2027, with no specific release date shared for the upcoming Witcher 3 expansion.

It’s been rumored for months now that a new Witcher 3 expansion was in the works, with a May 2026 release window at the heart of the leaks. Of course, with the news breaking in May, that couldn’t be further from the truth, and instead, we’re left guessing what time next year the long-awaited DLC will arrive.

  • More Insider Gaming: Best Sony PlayStation Days of Play 2026 Deals: End Date & Sale Games

Given that it’s being co-developed by Fools Theory, a team filled with Witcher veterans and behind Thaumaturge, the DLC shouldn’t interrupt the development of The Witcher 4. Instead, it should be a nice bonus, and it’s even rumored to bridge the gap between the third and fourth game.

When Will We See More Of The Witcher 3 Songs Of The Past?

According to CD Projekt Red, we can expect to see more of Songs of the Past late this Summer.

As things stand, not much is known about the upcoming expansion, other than its release window and name. Naturally, fans want to know exactly what’s in store for them next year with Songs of the Past, but you might have to wait a while to find out.

  • More Insider Gaming: All 007: First Light Cameos—Every Celebrity Cameo in 007: First Light

Summer typically ends around late August/early September, so it’s safe to assume new details will emerge around then. With Gamescom Opening Night Live taking place on August 25, is there a chance a trailer could debut there? Time will surely tell.

Are you excited for Songs of the Past? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more Insider Gaming content, check out the following:

  • Xbox Games Showcase 2026 & Gears of War: E-Day Direct
  • Summer Game Fest 2026 Showcase—Date & Start Times
  • PlayStation State Of Play June 2026 Showcase

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

MOBILE APP

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: KRAFTON Agrees To Pay Subnautica Devs Up To $250 Million, It’s Reported
item.content:encoded:
A new report has revealed that Subnautica 2’s sales have led to Krafton agreeing to pay the promised earnout of up to $250 million.

For those unaware, there was a long legal battle between Krafton and the developers of Subnautica 2 over the earnout, in which the devs claimed Krafton was intentionally delaying the release, while Krafton alleged that the execs had abandoned the title. The lawsuit eventually led to the Subnautica 2 CEO being reinstated, and now, it seems the promised earnout will be provided as well.

Subnautica 2’s Spectacular Sales Will Reportedly Lead to Krafton Paying the Promised Earnout

As spotted by IGN, a new report by Korean Economic Daily states that their game industry sources have learned that Krafton has agreed to pay Subnautica 2’s earnout of up to $250 million to the shareholders. The South Korean company will reportedly have to pay “$3.12 for every $1, or up to $250 million, to Unknown Worlds’ former shareholders every time the studio’s revenue topped $69.8 million a month since the Korean game developer acquired the US firm in 2021.” The $250 million sum was about 35% of its operating profit last year.

Subnautica 2 has had impressive sales, reaching 1 million copies sold on its first day, even while still in early access. It also significantly surpassed the previous game’s all-time player peak, nearing 500,000 concurrent players. To date, the title has sold over 4 million copies and, according to an Alinea Analytics report, generated $100 million in a week and currently holds the record for the fastest-selling game on Steam for 2026.

In other news, Subnautica 2 developers, Unknown Worlds, have revealed the three areas they are focusing on for early access. Additionally, Unknown Worlds revealed the Subnautica 2 Early Access roadmap, teasing co-op upgrades, new biomes, vehicles, and story content. What are your thoughts on Subnautica 2 devs getting the promised earnout of up to $250 million from Krafton? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Fortnite showing the first 10 minutes of The Mandalorian and Grogu for free. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.
